mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack signals impending corporate earnings deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a synchronized collapse in consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative SPX 60-day returns, with a combined regression coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, an accelerating industrial production index reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hikes, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive SPX 20-day returns during tightening periods, or the predictive coefficient turns negative with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit margins and dividend payouts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show no statistically significant correlation with SPX 5-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 205.0, inflation expectations force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in over 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 205.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to predict positive SPX 20-day returns, with a joint F-test p-value exceeding 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, declining housing starts signal deteriorating construction sector demand and reduced household wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to predict negative SPX 5-day returns during late-cycle periods, or the signal reverses direction with statistical significance (p < 0.05).
